public_goals: Reach a workable separation structure without prolonged relational conflict.
Show willingness to acknowledge the other party's experience without re-litigating what happened.
Protect the children from exposure to ongoing relational conflict between the parents.
private_concerns: Worries that any acknowledgment they offer will be used as an admission or attacked further.
Feels that prolonged emotional processing will prevent practical progress for both parties.
Is genuinely uncertain how to be present to the other party's pain without making it worse.
red_lines: Will not accept a process framed primarily as accountability or blame.
Will not agree to a separation structure that requires relitigating the relational history in every session.
emotional_triggers: the process being used to relitigate rather than move forward
their willingness to acknowledge being treated as insufficient or insincere
children's needs being subordinated to relational processing
disclosure_tendencies: offers acknowledgment early but keeps it bounded
becomes more practical-focused once acknowledgment has been received without attack
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes more defensive if the process becomes accountability-focused rather than forward-looking
shows more flexibility when acknowledgment is accepted and the process moves toward structure
starting_positions: Primary position: the process should move toward a workable separation structure without requiring relational resolution first.
Secondary position: acknowledgment of what happened is appropriate, but should not be the primary session focus.
likely_openings: I want to do this right. I know what I did affected things.
I am here to work toward something workable for both of us, especially for the children.
